---
title: "City of Albuquerque Annual Cost of Service Study and Rate Setting"
year: 2025
image: /images/albuquerque.jpg
image-alt: "Image of Albuquerque"
categories: ["Rate Setting", "Integrated Solid Waste Planning"]
---
{{< include _return-to-pf.qmd >}}

::: {.article}
![](/images/albuquerque.jpg){.left-img}

# 2009-2025 Annual Cost of Service Study and Rate Setting

Bell & Associates has assisted the City of Albuquerque’s Solid Waste Management
Department with calculating the cost of service rates for the City’s 180,000
residential and 11,000 commercial customers, transfer facilities, Clean City
programs, and the Cerro Colorado Landfill since 2009. In the past, rate
adjustments had been made by applying a percentage across all service fees.
This method resulted in some customers paying disproportionately into the
system and actually subsidizing the services of other customers. These
subsidies were not based on the Administration or City Council’s decision that
it was a beneficial policy, but merely on a faulty rate structure. The cost of
service study identified these rates and fully accounted for the Department’s
costs in the service fees.

The process coincides with the annual budget review and provides the Solid
Waste Department with themeans to present program changes and rate adjustments
for the upcoming fiscal year to the City Council. Mr. Bell has completed the
financial review and cost modeling of the current system and projected future
costs of collection, disposal rates, and service fees for the diverse services
provided by the City. 

# 2017 Transfer Station 25-year Cost of Service Analysis

# 2016 Solid Waste Management Plan Update

TODO: Contents

[Thumbnail Image by Quintin Soloviev - Own work, CC BY 4.0](https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=186809197)
:::
